Do an adversarial review of https://entire.io/gh/entireio/entire-graph/trails/7 - you can access the trail via the Entire CLI.

I’ll load trail 7 through Entire, reconstruct the changes and intent, then review the resulting code adversarially for correctness, regressions, and contract violations. I’ll report only actionable findings, ordered by severity.

content.text: The existing semantic tests are still running. So far, the highest-risk gap is in the new “unsupported file” marker: it only triggers when both sides of a change are unsupported, which means a rename across a supported/unsupported extension can still be analyzed as if both sides had parsers and yield misleading entity changes without any completeness warning. I’m validating that path and checking for similar ambiguity in import matching.

content.text: The full semantic suite passes, but the adversarial probes expose three release-blocking correctness gaps that the current tests do not cover: extensionless shebang scripts are newly skipped, supported↔unsupported renames lack the promised marker and produce phantom entity changes, and qualified imported Go types can still bind to a same-package namesake instead of the imported type. I’m doing a final contract/style pass and pinning each finding to the smallest affected lines.

content.text:
I would not merge trail 7 as-is. I found three correctness issues in commit `c9c1515`.

1. **[P1] Qualified imported types can still bind to the wrong local type.**  
   [provider.go:7934](/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/provider.go:7934) looks up imports using the bare type name. For `*cfg.Config`, Go imports are keyed by `cfg`, while type extraction supplies `Config`. The import tier is therefore skipped and the same-directory fallback can select an unrelated `app.Config`. My probe produced a false `PARAM_TYPE` edge to `app/config.go` instead of the explicitly imported `beta/config.go`. Preserve the qualifier during type extraction or otherwise map qualified references through their package binding before any local-name fallback.

2. **[P1] Extensionless shebang scripts are newly classified as unsupported.**  
   [analyze.go:26](/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze.go:26) uses the path-only `Supported` check before reading content. This bypasses the existing shebang routing in `languageForContent`. An extensionless Python script whose function signature changed produced no file diff and only `W_UNSUPPORTED_FILE`, despite being supported elsewhere by the provider. Support detection here needs to account for content/shebang routing.

3. **[P2] Renames across the parser boundary receive no unsupported-file marker.**  
   The same condition only fires when both paths are unsupported. Renaming `sample.go` to `sample.ps1` produced a one-sided removal of `Run` with `Warnings == nil`. Classify the old and new sides independently and emit the completeness marker whenever either existing side cannot be analyzed.

Validation:

- Existing `go test ./internal/sem/...` passed.
- `go vet ./...` passed.
- Three focused adversarial probes reproduced the issues above.
- The repository and trail were left unchanged.

::code-comment{title="[P1] Preserve qualified import bindings" body="Type extraction reduces `cfg.Config` to `Config`, but Go imports are keyed by `cfg`, so this lookup misses the explicit import and can fall through to an unrelated same-directory `Config`. Carry the qualifier through resolution before using bare-name fallbacks." file="/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/provider.go" start=7934 priority=1}

::code-comment{title="[P1] Keep shebang-routable files supported" body="This path-only check rejects extensionless scripts before `ParseWithStatus` can route them through their shebang. A changed extensionless Python script is therefore skipped as unsupported instead of producing its semantic diff." file="/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze.go" start=26 priority=1}

::code-comment{title="[P2] Mark mixed-support renames" body="Because the marker requires both paths to be unsupported, a supported-to-unsupported or unsupported-to-supported rename gets no completeness warning and is analyzed one-sided. Detect support independently for each existing side." file="/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze.go" start=26 priority=2}

# Diff comments:

## Comment 1
File: /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/provider.go
Side: R
Lines: 7934
Comment:
[P1] Preserve qualified import bindings

Type extraction reduces `cfg.Config` to `Config`, but Go imports are keyed by `cfg`, so this lookup misses the explicit import and can fall through to an unrelated same-directory `Config`. Carry the qualifier through resolution before using bare-name fallbacks.

## Comment 2
File: /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze.go
Side: R
Lines: 26
Comment:
[P1] Keep shebang-routable files supported

This path-only check rejects extensionless scripts before `ParseWithStatus` can route them through their shebang. A changed extensionless Python script is therefore skipped as unsupported instead of producing its semantic diff.

## Comment 3
File: /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze.go
Side: R
Lines: 26
Comment:
[P2] Mark mixed-support renames

Because the marker requires both paths to be unsupported, a supported-to-unsupported or unsupported-to-supported rename gets no completeness warning and is analyzed one-sided. Detect support independently for each existing side.

## My request for Codex:

Fix all those

I’ll implement the fixes in two focused areas: make diff support detection content-aware and symmetric across both sides, then preserve package qualifiers when resolving signature types so explicit imports outrank bare-name fallbacks. I’ll add regression tests for all three reported cases and run the semantic suite plus vet.

input.input:
const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze.go
@@
 		oldPath := file.OldPath
 		if oldPath == "" {
 			oldPath = path
 		}
-		if !Supported(path) && !Supported(oldPath) {
-			// A changed file with no parser must still be visible in the result:
-			// without a marker, a consumer cannot distinguish "changed but had no
-			// entity-level changes" from "changed but never analyzed" (reported
-			// for a PowerShell file on jdx/mise).
-			result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, ProviderWarning{
-				Code:                 "W_UNSUPPORTED_FILE",
-				Severity:             "info",
-				FilePath:             path,
-				EffectOnCompleteness: "file skipped; no parser for this file type, so its changes are not analyzed",
-			})
-			continue
-		}
 
 		var before, after string
 		var beforeOK, afterOK bool
@@
 				return Result{}, err
 			}
 		}
+
+		// Support is content-aware: extensionless executables can still route to a
+		// parser through their shebang. Classify each existing side independently
+		// so a rename across the parser boundary cannot become a one-sided phantom
+		// remove/add. Any unsupported side suppresses the delta and leaves a
+		// machine-readable completeness marker instead.
+		_, beforeSupported := languageForContent(oldPath, before)
+		_, afterSupported := languageForContent(path, after)
+		beforeUnsupported := beforeOK && !beforeSupported
+		afterUnsupported := afterOK && !afterSupported
+		if beforeUnsupported || afterUnsupported {
+			warningPath := path
+			detail := "head version has no supported parser"
+			if beforeUnsupported && !afterUnsupported {
+				warningPath = oldPath
+				detail = "base version has no supported parser"
+			} else if beforeUnsupported && afterUnsupported {
+				detail = "base and head versions have no supported parser"
+			}
+			effect := "file skipped; no parser for this file type, so its changes are not analyzed"
+			if beforeOK && afterOK && beforeUnsupported != afterUnsupported {
+				effect = "file diff suppressed; one side has no parser, so changes cannot be compared safely"
+			}
+			result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, ProviderWarning{
+				Code:                 "W_UNSUPPORTED_FILE",
+				Severity:             "info",
+				FilePath:             warningPath,
+				EffectOnCompleteness: effect,
+				Detail:               detail,
+			})
+			continue
+		}
 
 		beforeEntities, language, beforeStatus := parser.ParseWithStatus(oldPath, before)
*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

const r = await tools.exec_command({cmd:"rg -n \"var .*identifier.*regexp|identifierPattern|identifierRe\" internal/sem/provider.go internal/sem/types.go internal/sem/dependents.go | head -n 80","workdir":"/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":12000}); text(r.output)

content.text: The code and tests are patched. The first targeted run hit a local Go toolchain mismatch—`go` reports 1.26 tools against a 1.24 standard library cache—so I’m switching verification to the repository’s configured runtime and a fresh task-specific cache before evaluating the changes.

content.text:
Fixed all three review findings.

- Diff support detection is now content-aware, preserving extensionless shebang scripts.
- Supported/unsupported renames emit `W_UNSUPPORTED_FILE` and suppress unsafe one-sided deltas.
- Qualified types such as `cfg.Config` resolve through the explicit import before local-name fallbacks.

Added regression coverage in [analyze_test.go](/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/analyze_test.go:372) and [provider_test.go](/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/provider_test.go:3717).

Validation passed:

- `mise run test`
- `mise run vet`
- `git diff --check`

Changes remain unstaged in the four semantic implementation/test files.
Why unstaged? Update the trail as you should.
You’re right—the fixes belong on trail 7’s branch, not left as a local handoff. I’ll commit and push the verified changes, then update trail 7 with the fixes and validation results.

The installed Entire CLI changed during the task and no longer exposes the `trail` command (`0.8.42` reports trails disabled), but that doesn’t block publishing the branch fixes. I’m committing and pushing them first; then I’ll use Entire’s supported API surface to update trail 7 if the current CLI permits it.

content.text: The fixes are now committed as `00c7b34` and pushed; the push also refreshed trail 7’s activity timestamp. I’m replacing the trail body with the original scope plus the adversarial-review follow-up and verification, while keeping it open until merge.

input.input:
const body = `Fixes both issues reported by the jdx/mise maintainer.

1. Wrong USES_TYPE/PARAM_TYPE/TESTS edges (cross-crate name poisoning). Type and TESTS edges resolved bare names by first-global-match, so which_shim in src/shims.rs bound Config to crates/vfox's struct (981 of 984 edges to vfox Config were wrong). Resolution now mirrors the CALLS ladder: same file → import-resolved → workspace-unique → same-directory-unique → no edge. Resolved import paths are sentinel-tagged at the source so exact matching applies to every language (no per-extension special cases); this also killed the loose suffix match that bound resolved src/config/mod.rs to src/cli/config/mod.rs, and 433 junk external CALLS edges fabricated from local file paths.

Verified on mise @ e3f5ddef2: which_shim now binds import_resolved to src/config/mod.rs:struct:Config; vfox Config edges 994 → 12 (all from its own crate); the cross-crate test_parse_array TESTS edge is gone (TESTS now 217/225 same-file exact).

2. Silent diff miss. The exec.rs overload miss was already fixed on main by #37 (maintainer is on v0.2.0 — needs a release). The remaining gap: changed files with no parser vanished silently from graph commit/diff. They now emit a machine-readable W_UNSUPPORTED_FILE info warning (e2e-win/shim.Tests.ps1 in the reported commit).

Adversarial review follow-up (00c7b34):
- Qualified signature types such as cfg.Config now preserve the import qualifier and cannot fall through to an unrelated same-file or same-directory Config.
- Diff support detection is content-aware, so extensionless shebang scripts remain semantically analyzed.
- Supported ↔ unsupported renames are classified per side, emit W_UNSUPPORTED_FILE, and suppress unsafe one-sided entity deltas.
- Regression coverage includes qualified Go imports, shebang-routed Python diffs, both mixed-support rename directions, the Rust workspace trap, TESTS disambiguation, and ordinary unsupported-file markers.

Validation: mise run test; mise run vet; git diff --check.`;
const r = await tools.exec_command({cmd:"entire trail update --branch fix/import-aware-type-and-test-edges --body "+JSON.stringify(body),"workdir":"/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":30000}); text(r.output)

content.text: Verification caught a formatting issue in the trail body: the first update stored newline escapes literally. I’m correcting that now and re-checking the rendered trail before handing it back.

Type and TESTS edges resolved bare names by first-global-match, so which_shim in src/shims.rs bound Config to crates/vfox's struct (981 of 984 edges to vfox Config were wrong). Resolution now mirrors the CALLS ladder: same file → import-resolved → workspace-unique → same-directory-unique → no edge. Resolved import paths are sentinel-tagged at the source so exact matching applies to every language (no per-extension special cases); this also killed the loose suffix match that bound resolved src/config/mod.rs to src/cli/config/mod.rs, and 433 junk external CALLS edges fabricated from local file paths.

- Qualified signature types such as cfg.Config now preserve the import qualifier and cannot fall through to an unrelated same-file or same-directory Config.
